subject: UX & Research
title: Survivorship Bias in Analytics
description: A micro-lesson that makes Survivorship Bias in Analytics usable.

hook.text: Your analytics show users love the checkout flow. But you're only measuring those who reached checkout.

buildup.text: In WWII, engineers armored bullet-riddled planes. Wald said: armor the untouched spots — those planes crashed.

discovery.text: Survivorship bias means studying winners and ignoring the dead. Analytics only shows users who stayed.

twist.text: Users who leave in the first 5 seconds rarely appear in analytics. Your data only reflects survivors.

climax.text: Track drop-off points, not just success paths. The users you can't see are the ones you need most.

punchline.text: Don't just study winners. The users who left tell the real story.

quiz.question: How does survivorship bias distort UX metrics?
options: Analytics only tracks users who stayed — ignoring those who left before being measured
Survivorship bias only affects financial metrics
